Title: Genome-scale analysis of the cotton KCS gene family revealed a binary mode of action for gibberellin A regulated fiber growth

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides structural cell wall material contributing to thicker cotton fiber walls and higher dry matter accumulation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Four GhCesA genes with GAREs/L1 boxes were significantly upregulated by GA3; two major CesAs lacking these elements were not induced
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter motif analysis; qRT-PCR after GA3, C26:0 and ethylene treatments; cellulose quantification; electron microscopy
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous gibberellin A3 (1 μM); exogenous C26:0 (5 μM); exogenous ethylene (2.2 ppm)
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GA3 induced these CesA genes and increased fiber cell wall thickness and cellulose content; C26:0 induced two CesA genes moderately; ethylene did not induce CesA expression
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Four GhCesA genes contained GAREs and/or L1 boxes in their promoters. 2. All four were significantly induced by GA3. 3. GA3 treatment caused stronger fiber cell wall thickening and cellulose accumulation than C26:0 or ethylene, placing CesA activation in a GA downstream branch.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper emphasizes GA acts downstream toward cell wall synthesis in parallel with the upstream VLCFA-ethylene pathway; individual CesA gene IDs reported in part as CesA6-4 and CesA6-7 for C26:0 response, but full core-gene-specific IDs for all four GA-responsive CesAs were not fully specified in the text excerpt
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoters with GAREs and/or L1 boxes

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: KCS family, ELO-type very-long-chain fatty acid elongase condensing enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the first condensation step of VLCFA elongation, producing β-ketoacyl-CoA from an acyl-CoA and malonyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports cotton fiber elongation and contributes to downstream cell wall/cellulose deposition during fiber development
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing cotton fiber cells; yeast cellular lipid fraction
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fiber-specific in cotton; functionally active in yeast complementation system
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream, first committed condensation step in VLCFA elongation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for VLCFA-mediated fiber growth
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast elo2Δelo3Δ complementation; growth assay; GC-MS fatty acid profiling; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Restored viability of yeast elo2Δelo3Δ mutant and supported accumulation of near wild-type C26:0, indicating promotion of VLCFA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhKCS12 rescued the lethal growth phenotype of yeast elo2Δelo3Δ. 2. Complemented yeast accumulated almost wild-type levels of C26:0. 3. The gene showed fiber-specific expression, supporting a role in cotton fiber development.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step supported as first step of VLCFA biosynthesis, but specific rate-limiting status for GhKCS12 not established
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Yeast ADH promoter (constitutive) in complementation assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as ELO-type KCS in this paper; one of four ELO-type cotton KCSs showing strong complementation efficiency

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: KCS family, ELO-type very-long-chain fatty acid elongase condensing enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the condensation of acyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to form β-ketoacyl-CoA during VLCFA elongation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports membrane/surface lipid elongation and cotton fiber development-associated VLCFA production
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream, condensation step of VLCFA elongation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for VLCFA-mediated growth processes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast complementation; growth assay; GC-MS fatty acid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Restored viability of elo2Δelo3Δ yeast and produced almost wild-type C26:0 levels
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhKCS14 functionally replaced yeast Elo2/Elo3 activity. 2. Complemented yeast showed close-to-wild-type growth. 3. VLCFA profiling indicated production up to C26:0.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of four ELO-type GhKCS genes with strong complementation

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: KCS family, ELO-type very-long-chain fatty acid elongase condensing enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes β-ketoacyl-CoA formation in the VLCFA elongation cycle from acyl-CoA and malonyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with VLCFA production needed for growth processes
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream, condensation step of VLCFA elongation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast complementation; growth assay; GC-MS fatty acid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Restored viability of elo2Δelo3Δ yeast and supported near wild-type C26:0 accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhKCS53 complemented yeast elo2Δelo3Δ lethality. 2. It supported near wild-type growth. 3. It enabled VLCFA synthesis up to C26:0.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: KCS family, ELO-type very-long-chain fatty acid elongase condensing enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the first condensation reaction in VLCFA elongation, generating β-ketoacyl-CoA from acyl-CoA and malonyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports cotton fiber elongation and associated lipid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing cotton fiber cells; yeast cellular lipid fraction
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fiber-specific in cotton; functionally active in yeast complementation system
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for fiber growth-promoting VLCFAs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast complementation; growth assay; GC-MS fatty acid profiling; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Restored yeast mutant viability and enabled near wild-type C26:0 accumulation; implicated in cotton fiber development
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhKCS26 strongly rescued the lethal elo2Δelo3Δ yeast phenotype. 2. Complemented cells accumulated almost wild-type levels of C26:0. 3. The gene is fiber-specific, supporting a role in cotton fiber development.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step supported as part of first step of VLCFA biosynthesis, but specific rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of four ELO-type genes with high complementation efficiency

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely catalyzes elongation from C20:0 to C22:0 by condensing C20 acyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports VLCFA diversity relevant to cotton development
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in flower; flower-specific high expression associated with promoter changes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functionally active in yeast complementation system
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream elongation step with chain-length specificity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast complementation; growth assay; GC-MS fatty acid profiling; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Partially rescued yeast mutant and caused strong accumulation of C22:0 with little/no longer VLCFAs, indicating chain-length-specific activity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhKCS18 rescued elo2Δelo3Δ yeast with lower efficiency than ELO-type genes. 2. Complemented yeast accumulated high C22:0 but undetectable C24:0/C26:0. 3. The gene is highly expressed in flower, indicating specialized developmental function.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch/chain-length determinant rather than established rate-limiting enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May bias flux toward shorter VLCFAs rather than C26:0
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Depletion of two WRKY binding sites in DtKCS18 promoter may underlie high flower-specific expression

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely catalyzes elongation from C22:0 to C24:0 by condensing C22 acyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports VLCFA diversity associated with lipid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream elongation step with chain-length specificity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast complementation; growth assay; GC-MS fatty acid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Partially rescued yeast mutant and caused accumulation of C24:0 with negligible C26:0, indicating specificity for C24:0 production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhKCS20 rescued elo2Δelo3Δ yeast less efficiently than the strong ELO-type genes. 2. Complemented yeast accumulated high C24:0 with little C26:0. 3. The gene likely catalyzes the C22:0 to C24:0 elongation step.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch/chain-length determinant rather than established rate-limiting enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May divert flux away from C26:0 accumulation

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Inferred KCS condensation step in VLCFA elongation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes cotton fiber elongation through VLCFA pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Preferentially expressed during fiber growth stages
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Rapidly induced by exogenous GA3; promoter contains three consecutive GAREs
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for VLCFA-ethylene pathway involved in fiber elongation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter motif analysis; qRT-PCR after GA3 treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Transcript increased about 30-fold after GA3 treatment, implicating it in GA-activated VLCFA biosynthesis during fiber growth
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhKCS28 is a fiber-stage-preferential KCS gene. 2. Its promoter contains three consecutive GAREs. 3. GA3 induced its transcript by about 30-fold, linking GA signaling to VLCFA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter with three consecutive GAREs
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A Copia retrotransposon insertion was found in DtKCS28 promoter

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Inferred KCS condensation step in VLCFA elongation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes cotton fiber elongation through VLCFA pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Preferentially expressed during fiber growth stages
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Rapidly induced by exogenous GA3; promoter contains three consecutive GAREs
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for VLCFA-ethylene pathway involved in fiber elongation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter motif analysis; qRT-PCR after GA3 treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Transcript increased about 30-fold after GA3 treatment, supporting a role in GA-stimulated VLCFA production during fiber development
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhKCS23 is preferentially expressed during fiber growth. 2. Its promoter contains three consecutive GAREs. 3. GA3 induced its transcript by about 30-fold.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter with three consecutive GAREs

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Inferred KCS condensation step in VLCFA elongation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes cotton fiber elongation through VLCFA pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Preferentially expressed during fiber growth stages
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced 20–30 fold by GA3; promoter contains one GARE and one L1 box
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for fiber elongation pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter motif analysis; qRT-PCR after GA3 treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly induced by GA3, linking it to GA-promoted VLCFA biosynthesis in fibers
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhKCS13 contains one GARE and one L1 box in its promoter. 2. GA3 induced its transcript by about 20–30 fold. 3. It is part of the GA-responsive KCS set during fiber development.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter with one GARE and one L1 box

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Inferred KCS condensation step in VLCFA elongation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes cotton fiber elongation through VLCFA pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Preferentially expressed during fiber growth stages
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced 20–30 fold by GA3; promoter contains one GARE and one L1 box
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for fiber elongation pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter motif analysis; qRT-PCR after GA3 treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly induced by GA3, supporting a role in GA-responsive VLCFA biosynthesis during fiber growth
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhKCS38 contains one GARE and one L1 box in its promoter. 2. GA3 induced its transcript by about 20–30 fold. 3. Point mutations created additional MYC binding sites associated with fiber-specific expression.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter with one GARE and one L1 box
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Additional MYC binding sites on AtKCS38 promoter may contribute to high fiber-specific expression

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Inferred KCS condensation step in VLCFA elongation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes cotton fiber elongation through VLCFA pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Preferentially expressed during fiber growth stages
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Significantly induced by GA3; promoter contains two GAREs
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for fiber elongation pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter motif analysis; qRT-PCR after GA3 treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Significantly induced by GA3, implicating it in GA-activated VLCFA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhKCS36 is among the fiber-preferential KCS genes. 2. Its promoter contains two GAREs. 3. GA3 significantly induced its expression.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Inferred KCS condensation step in VLCFA elongation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes cotton fiber elongation through VLCFA pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Preferentially expressed during fiber growth stages
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Significantly induced by GA3; promoter contains one distal GARE
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for fiber elongation pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter motif analysis; qRT-PCR after GA3 treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Significantly induced by GA3, linking it to hormonal activation of VLCFA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhKCS37 has a GARE in its promoter located >1 kb upstream. 2. GA3 significantly induced its transcription. 3. It belongs to the GA-responsive KCS cohort during fiber development.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter with one distal GARE

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Significantly induced by GA3, supporting a role in GA-activated fiber VLCFA metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhKCS40 has a distal GARE in its promoter. 2. GA3 significantly induced its expression. 3. It is included among fiber-stage-preferential KCS genes.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Inferred KCS condensation step in VLCFA elongation

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Preferentially expressed during fiber growth stages

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhKCS1 has a GARE in its promoter. 2. GA3 significantly induced its transcription. 3. Additional MYB binding sites on DtKCS1 promoter may contribute to fiber-specific expression.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter with one distal GARE
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Two new MYB binding sites were generated on DtKCS1 promoter by point mutations
